titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Tony Gleaton Photographs LSC.2481
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Kelly Besser, 2026.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 March 18.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-14 13:44:39 -0700.

did.abstract: Tony Gleaton was an African American photographer. Born in Detroit, Michigan, he moved with his family to California at the age of 11. He joined the Marines after high school, then pursued a college degree at the University of California Los Angeles (UCLA), where he became interested in photography. A large portion of his work was about capturing images of African displacement in the West (the Americas) and its influence. He was also well known for his photography of Black cowboys. This collection spans the 1980s and contains three vintage silver gelatin photographs by Gleaton.
did.physloc: Held at UCLA Library Special Collections. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Reproduction and Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to the UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of John MacDonald Pitblado and Octavio O. Olvera, 2023.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Tony Gleaton photographs (Collection 2481). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's Library Collections and Archives.

bioghist.head: Administrative/Biographical History
bioghist.p: Tony Gleaton (August 4, 1948 – August 14, 2015) was an African American photographer. Born in Detroit, Michigan, he moved with his family to California at the age of 11. He joined the Marines after high school, then pursued a college degree at the University of California Los Angeles, where he became interested in photography. A large portion of his work was about capturing images of African displacement in the West (the Americas) and its influence. He was also well known for his photography of Black cowboys. Gleaton died in his home in Palo Alto, California, on August 14, 2015, after a long battle with oral cancer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: This collection spans the 1980s and contains three vintage silver gelatin photographs by Tony Gleaton.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The existing order of materials was maintained.

did.unittitle: "Picking a mount" signed silver gelatin print
"Suenos de caballos" signed silver gelatin print
